steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Use corresponding angles
steps.content: In similar triangles, corresponding angles are always equal. Since $A$ corresponds to $D$, we have $\angle D = \angle A = 26^{\circ}$. Statement I is true.

steps.title: Check the side statement
steps.content: Similar triangles have proportional sides, not necessarily equal sides. $AB$ corresponds to $DE$, but the scale factor is unknown. $DE$ could equal 4, 8, 2, or any other positive value. Statement II is not necessarily true.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: I only — Statement I ($\angle D = 26^{\circ}$) must be true; Statement II ($DE = 4$) need not be true. That matches Choice A — I only.
  • Choice B: II only — Statement I is definitely true and Statement II is not guaranteed. Eliminated.
  • Choice C: I and II — Statement II is not necessarily true. Eliminated.
  • Choice D: Neither I nor II — Statement I must be true. Eliminated.
